spec_match.rationale: Parses the MIME header from a dropped file, offers parameter injection when OS-supplied MIME strings have no parameters, lists each parameter with a v146 preservation verdict, builds before-vs-after data URLs, and renders the result across ``, ``, `

interactive.rationale: File input upload, four presets (PDF, UTF-8 CSV, ISO-8859-1 CSV, JPEG), a no-parameter injection affordance, parameters table, before/after URL toggle, render grid, and corruption comparison all update live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises file drop + FileReader, parameters table with v146-vs-pre-v146, before/after toggle, presets, and a render test across img / a-download / iframe; the page now also shows the corruption that dropped charset parameters cause.

edge_cases.rationale: The real-file branch now detects the common no-parameter case and offers an injected parameter string for text, CSV, PDF, and JPEG file types. The Latin-1 CSV preset shows the concrete mojibake failure when `charset=iso-8859-1` is stripped and the correct decoded output when it is preserved.

references.rationale: The page now includes a see-also section linking to the feature page, sibling concepts, ChromeStatus feature 4874471565557760, the Fetch spec data-URL section, and MDN data: URLs.

design_system.rationale: Badge styles use design-system variables (`accent-emerald`, `accent-rose`, `accent-blue`) and the added suggestion/corruption UI uses the existing token palette. Buttons with custom backgrounds also set text color.

summary: The page now handles the real OS-file-picker path and the visible corruption case: Chrome Canary 151 via chrome-devtools-mcp uploaded a CSV with no MIME parameters, showed the injection affordance, applied `text/csv; charset=utf-8; header=present`, and loaded the Latin-1 CSV preset showing UTF-8 fallback mojibake versus preserved `iso-8859-1` decoding with no console or network failures.
